dna: Stylized 2D animation frame in the Bruce Timm DC-Animated art-deco lineage (Batman: The Animated Series grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 90s-2000s American TV animation, dark-deco. Named anchor: Timm graphic shadow simplification — bold flat shapes, black backgrounds, art-deco geometry. Signature light: dramatic single-source noir-graphic light throwing hard-edged black shadow shapes; the shadow is a flat graphic mass, not a gradient; a subject often lit against a pure black ground with a single rim. Color/grade: limited bold palette on black — deco-desaturated with saturated accent (a red, a yellow); painted on black cel so the darks are true black. Lens/optics: flat 2D, low dramatic deco angles, strong graphic composition; no lens simulation. Texture/render: bold clean varied-weight black line, flat cel with hard-edged shadow shapes, art-deco architectural geometry (chunky, stylized, angular), simplified planar faces. Composition+motion: strong graphic silhouette against black, deco-angular staging, snappy limited-animation motion with held dramatic poses; the flat shadow shape and deco geometry carry the mood.
anchor: Timm dark-deco: bold flat shapes on true black, hard-edged graphic shadow masses, deco-angular architecture, limited desaturated palette with one saturated accent, dramatic single-source rim, snappy held poses.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Bruce Timm's art-deco graphic-shadow simplification and painted-on-black drama onto the chosen world; use for a bold, noir-graphic, deco-stylized beat.
avoid: NO Batman, NO Joker, NO Harley Quinn, NO named DC character, NO bat-emblem or franchise costume. NO Gotham named landmark. NO gradient shading, NO photoreal render, NO cluttered detail, NO soft light.

dna: Graphic-design frame in the Bauhaus geometric-hierarchy lineage (functional-modernist grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 1920s-30s Bauhaus design language as a composition and color doctrine. Named anchor: Bauhaus primary-geometry hierarchy — form follows function, circle/square/triangle, primary color logic. Signature light: none simulated — pure flat graphic; value contrast comes from the flat color relationships, not from lighting. Color/grade: Bauhaus primaries — red, blue, yellow on black and white, plus grey; disciplined, limited, functional; each color assigned by hierarchy, never decorative. Lens/optics: flat orthographic composition, an asymmetric-but-balanced grid, diagonal tension against horizontal/vertical structure; no perspective, no DOF. Texture/render: pure flat geometric shapes — precise circles, squares, triangles, hard clean edges, no gradient, no texture; typographic grid-locked sans-serif. Composition+motion: functional geometric hierarchy — the composition reads by size, position, and color weight on a grid; a shape snaps to its structural position; the balance of primary-color geometry IS the message.
anchor: Bauhaus geometric hierarchy: primary red/blue/yellow on black-white, precise circle/square/triangle, asymmetric-balanced grid with diagonal tension, flat functional color-by-hierarchy, grid-locked sans.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Bauhaus's functional primary-geometry hierarchy and grid discipline onto the chosen world; use for a rigorous, modernist, abstract-diagram or brand beat.
avoid: NO reproduction of a specific Bauhaus poster or its named designer's work. NO gradient, NO texture, NO 3D, NO photoreal element, NO decorative ornament (function only), NO organic anatomy.

dna: Stylized 2D animation frame in the Cartoon-Network graphic-snap lineage (bold flat TV-animation grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 2000s-2010s American TV animation, bold-flat comedic. Named anchor: CN graphic snap timing — thick clean outline, flat bold color, snappy squash-and-pop. Signature light: minimal flat lighting, occasional hard graphic shadow shape; bright and clear, value from flat color choice. Color/grade: bold saturated flat palette, clean and cheerful, thick black outline containing each shape; high readability. Lens/optics: flat 2D, bold graphic composition, exaggerated comedic staging; no lens simulation. Texture/render: thick clean uniform-to-varied black outline, flat solid color fills, no gradient, geometric-simplified character design; expressive rubber-hose-adjacent shapes. Composition+motion: snappy squash-and-stretch with a hard pop on the beat, quick held comedic poses, bold silhouette; the graphic snap and flat-bold clarity carry the energy.
anchor: CN graphic snap: thick clean black outline, flat bold saturated fills, geometric-simplified design, snappy squash-and-pop timing, hard comedic pose beats, high-readability silhouette.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Cartoon-Network's bold-flat graphic-snap timing and clean-outline clarity onto the chosen world; use for a punchy, comedic, bold-and-readable beat.
avoid: NO named Cartoon Network character (Finn, Jake, Gumball, etc.), NO franchise show design. NO gradient shading, NO photoreal render, NO painterly texture, NO muted grade, NO 3D.

dna: Graphic-design frame in the Russian Constructivist poster lineage (Rodchenko/Lissitzky force-line grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 1920s Soviet avant-garde design as a composition doctrine. Named anchor: Constructivist force-line drive — aggressive diagonals, photomontage, red-and-black propaganda energy. Signature light: none simulated — flat graphic with high-contrast photomontage elements; drama from the diagonal composition, not light. Color/grade: red, black, and cream/off-white — the Constructivist triad; bold, limited, ideological; occasional a single extra accent. Lens/optics: flat composition with extreme diagonal force-lines, a photomontage element cut in at an angle, radical asymmetry; no perspective DOF. Texture/render: flat bold shapes, hard diagonal bars and rays, high-contrast cut-in photomontage (halftone-photo fragment), heavy geometric sans-serif type at an angle; slight aged-print texture. Composition+motion: aggressive diagonal force-lines driving the eye, a photomontage fragment cutting across, radical asymmetric tension; a shape or bar drives in on the diagonal; the kinetic diagonal energy IS the message.
anchor: Constructivist force-line: aggressive diagonals and rays, red-black-cream triad, angled photomontage cut-in, radical asymmetry, heavy angled geometric sans, propaganda-kinetic tension.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Constructivism's diagonal force-line drive and red-black montage energy onto the chosen world; use for a bold, ideological, kinetic-diagonal poster beat.
avoid: NO reproduction of a specific Constructivist poster or Soviet propaganda slogan/leader. NO hammer-and-sickle or state emblem. NO gradient, NO photoreal 3D, NO decorative ornament, NO symmetric-calm composition.

dna: Stylized illustration frame in the Supergiant Hades / Jen Zee graphic-underworld lineage (painted-comic game-art grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: contemporary game illustration, hot graphic mythology. Named anchor: Jen Zee underworld heat — bold ink graphic forms with saturated painted heat-glow. Signature light: hot rim-and-glow lighting — a fiery underworld glow throwing saturated rim light on bold forms; hard graphic shadow with a warm-hot core; the light is stylized and hot, not naturalistic. Color/grade: saturated jewel-and-heat palette — hot red, molten orange, deep purple, cyan accent — rich and glowing against dark; bold comic-god saturation. Lens/optics: flat 2D illustration, dramatic low-angle heroic framing, bold graphic composition; painted depth. Texture/render: bold confident ink-comic line with painted rendering inside — saturated painted volume, hot glow-blooms, sharp graphic highlights; a painted-comic hybrid with god-like stylization. Composition+motion: bold heroic graphic staging, saturated heat-glow, a confident god-scale presence; a dramatic graphic pose held with painted heat; the hot-saturated ink-and-paint style carries the mythic energy.
anchor: Jen Zee underworld heat: bold ink-comic line with saturated painted volume, hot rim-and-glow lighting, molten jewel-heat palette against dark, god-scale heroic graphic staging, painted heat-glow.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Hades's hot-saturated painted-comic god-illustration grammar onto the chosen world; use for a bold, mythic, saturated-heat heroic beat.
avoid: NO Zagreus, NO Hades, NO named Supergiant Hades character, NO the specific game-god designs. NO franchise UI or logo. NO desaturated grade, NO photoreal render, NO flat-no-render vector, NO muted palette.

dna: Japanese woodblock print frame in the Hokusai/Hiroshige wave-plane lineage (ukiyo-e grammar, original composition only). Medium/era: Edo-period woodblock print as a composition and color doctrine. Named anchor: Hokusai wave-plane composition — bold flat color planes, the great diagonal wave-motif staging. Signature light: no directional lighting — value comes from the flat printed color fields, deepening only at plane edges. Color/grade: Prussian-blue dominant with aged cream-paper and a single madder-red seal accent; flat carved pigment, no blend. Lens/optics: flat print-plane composition, bold asymmetric diagonal wave-motif staging, deep foreground/background layering through flat planes rather than perspective; no lens. Texture/render: confident black key-block outline with irregular hand-carved edge, flat printed color separation with woodgrain bleed at edges, visible paper-grain and age-toning. Composition+motion: bold graphic flatness with a dominant diagonal wave or pattern plane, dramatic foreground overlap, a single sinusoidal motif pulse; the flat-plane layering and diagonal wave-energy carry the composition.
anchor: Hokusai wave-plane: bold flat Prussian-blue color planes, diagonal wave-motif staging, black key-block carved outline, deep flat-plane layering, madder-red seal accent, paper-grain age-toning.
use: Grammar only, original composition only — layers Hokusai's flat-plane wave-motif composition and woodblock color separation onto the chosen world; use for a bold, graphic, flat-plane classical-print beat.
avoid: NO direct reproduction of a specific famous print (e.g. The Great Wave off Kanagawa). NO named ukiyo-e artist signature or seal text. NO photographic gradient, NO 3D render, NO digital airbrush blend, NO modern glossy finish.

dna: Flat vector explainer frame in the Kurzgesagt system-clarity lineage (flat-2D grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: contemporary flat vector explainer animation. Named anchor: Kurzgesagt system clarity — flat solid shapes, systemic color-coding, the single insight-glow. Signature light: one soft amber insight-glow per beat, everything else flat-lit with no directional shadow; the glow blooms only at the moment of understanding. Color/grade: flat near-black navy-charcoal field, a single teal-cyan secondary, one warm amber insight accent; each conceptual element keeps one consistent flat color across the sequence. Lens/optics: locked overhead or 3/4 isometric orthographic view, no lens distortion, no DOF, no camera move beyond the diagram's reveal. Texture/render: flat solid-color fills with a 1-2px darker outline (never pure black), zero gradient except the insight-glow halo, geometric-simplified blob/capsule forms, soft drop-shadow depth-faking. Composition+motion: the isometric diagram expands from center, one connector line draws once to the insight node, the amber glow lands and holds; the diagram's reveal is the motion event; systemic clarity is the goal.
anchor: Kurzgesagt system clarity: flat solid shapes with 1-2px outline on near-black navy, systemic one-color-per-element coding, single amber insight-glow, locked isometric orthographic, diagram-reveal motion.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Kurzgesagt's flat-vector system-clarity and single-insight-glow grammar onto the chosen world; use for a clean, systemic, explainer-diagram beat.
avoid: NO Kurzgesagt bird mascot or any named explainer-channel character. NO photoreal texture, NO 3D render, NO painterly brushwork, NO gradient beyond the insight-glow, NO camera Dutch-tilt or handheld drift.

dna: Illustration frame in the medieval illuminated-manuscript lineage (gold-leaf hieratic grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: Gothic-medieval manuscript illumination as a composition and color doctrine. Named anchor: illuminated hieratic hierarchy — gold-leaf, flat hieratic scale, ornate border. Signature light: no naturalistic light — gold-leaf catches a real metallic gleam as the only 'light', everything else flat hieratic color; the gold IS the light. Color/grade: rich manuscript palette — lapis-blue, vermilion-red, gold-leaf, deep green on cream vellum; saturated jewel pigment, flat and hieratic. Lens/optics: flat hieratic composition, no perspective (hieratic scale — importance sets size, not distance), ornate decorated border framing the scene; no lens. Texture/render: fine confident ink outline, flat jewel-pigment fills, gold-leaf with a metallic gleam and tooled texture, ornate foliate border and decorated initial, vellum texture; hieratic flat figures. Composition+motion: hieratic scale hierarchy (the important figure largest, centered), an ornate border enclosing the scene, gold-leaf gleam; a stately, still, iconic composition; the flat hieratic grandeur and gold gleam carry the reverence.
anchor: Illuminated hieratic: gold-leaf metallic gleam as the only light, lapis-vermilion-gold jewel pigment on vellum, hieratic scale hierarchy, ornate foliate border, flat iconic stillness.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers the illuminated-manuscript's gold-leaf hieratic hierarchy and jewel-pigment grammar onto the chosen world; use for a reverent, ornate, iconic-flat historical beat.
avoid: NO reproduction of a specific historic manuscript or named saint/heraldry. NO religious-specific franchise iconography. NO photographic gradient, NO 3D render, NO naturalistic perspective, NO modern clean vector.

avoid: NO NASA logo, NO real mission insignia, NO named real spacecraft. NO reproduction of a specific Visions-of-the-Future poster. NO photoreal render, NO gradient-heavy digital, NO cluttered detail, NO cool-clinical grade.

dna: Stylized 2D animation frame in the Genndy Tartakovsky minimal lineage (Samurai Jack grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 2000s-2017 American TV animation, wide-format minimalism. Named anchor: Tartakovsky minimal staging — no outlines, shape and color, vast negative space, painted-background restraint. Signature light: bold graphic light-and-shadow shapes without outline; a strong single-source mood light casting a flat graphic shadow; the light shapes the form directly, no contour line. Color/grade: painterly-flat mood palette, a limited scheme per scene keyed to emotion, bold color blocks; atmospheric restraint. Lens/optics: flat 2D, ultra-wide compositions with vast negative space, a small figure in a large painted environment, strong horizontal staging; no lens DOF. Texture/render: NO character outlines — form carried entirely by shape and color separation; painted brush-textured backgrounds, flat graphic characters, deliberate restraint. Composition+motion: vast negative space with a small figure, ultra-wide painterly staging, long held stillness broken by a single decisive fast action; the minimalism and negative space carry the tension.
anchor: Tartakovsky minimal: no character outlines (shape and color only), vast negative space with a small figure, painted brush-textured backgrounds, emotion-keyed limited palette, held stillness then a decisive fast action.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Samurai Jack's outline-free minimal staging and vast negative-space restraint onto the chosen world; use for a bold, spare, tension-through-emptiness beat.
avoid: NO Samurai Jack, NO Aku, NO named Samurai Jack character or franchise design. NO character outlines (this style has none). NO cluttered detail, NO photoreal render, NO 3D, NO gradient-heavy digital.

dna: Illustration frame in the Ottoman Iznik folk-ornament lineage (tile-and-ceramic grammar, original composition only). Medium/era: 16th-century Iznik ceramic ornament as a composition and color doctrine. Named anchor: Iznik ornament rhythm — symmetric floral arabesque, cobalt-and-turquoise-and-coral, flat ceramic glaze. Signature light: no directional light — flat ceramic-glaze color, a subtle glaze sheen as the only surface event; value from the flat ornament color relationships. Color/grade: the Iznik palette — cobalt blue, turquoise, coral-bole red, sage green on white ground; saturated ceramic-glaze color, flat and jewel-like. Lens/optics: flat ornamental composition, symmetric or rhythmic-repeating arabesque layout, tile-panel framing; no perspective, no DOF. Texture/render: fine black-outline ornament, flat glaze-color fills with the characteristic raised coral-red, stylized tulip/carnation/hyacinth/rumi-arabesque motifs, a subtle ceramic sheen and craquelure. Composition+motion: symmetric rhythmic floral arabesque filling the frame, a repeating ornament pulse, tile-panel balance; the flat ceramic ornament rhythm and jewel-glaze color carry the decorative richness.

dna: Stylized animation story-DNA frame in an anime silhouette-discipline lineage (cel-grammar readability doctrine, original subjects only). Medium/era: general anime cel-animation staging as a readability discipline. Named anchor: silhouette-first discipline — every pose, prop, and character readable as a pure black silhouette. Signature light: strong separation light — a rim or backlight that carves the subject's silhouette cleanly against the ground; contre-jour and rim-separation favored so the shape reads instantly. Color/grade: whatever the world's grade, but staged for maximum silhouette contrast — subject-against-ground separation prioritized. Lens/optics: flat 2D, composed for silhouette clarity — the pose profiled or angled so the read is unambiguous, props held clear of the body-line; no DOF. Texture/render: confident cel line, but the discipline is compositional — poses designed so the silhouette alone communicates the action, hands and props clear of overlap. Composition+motion: silhouette-first staging — a held pose that reads as a clean black shape, action clear in profile, the negative space around the body kept legible; the readability of the silhouette is the craft, motion staged to keep every frame's shape clean.
anchor: Anime silhouette discipline: silhouette-first pose readability, rim/contre-jour separation carving the subject from ground, props clear of the body-line, action legible as a pure black shape, clean negative space.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers an anime silhouette-readability discipline onto the chosen world; use when a beat must read instantly and cleanly, staging every pose as a legible silhouette.
avoid: NO named anime character or franchise design. NO cluttered pose with props overlapping the body-line. NO flat frontal lighting that kills separation, NO ambiguous silhouette, NO 3D render.

dna: Stylized atmospheric frame in the Playdead Inside/Limbo liminal-shadow lineage (silhouette-minimalist game grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: contemporary atmospheric puzzle-game art direction, monochrome-leaning. Named anchor: liminal shadow minimalism — a small silhouette against a desaturated hazy void, dread through emptiness. Signature light: soft volumetric haze with a single directional source; the subject reads as a near-silhouette against a foggy desaturated ground; god-ray shafts and depth-haze; light is atmospheric and lonely. Color/grade: near-monochrome desaturation — grey, muted blue-grey, occasional dim sickly accent; almost colorless, atmospheric, oppressive. Lens/optics: flat 2D side-scroll-leaning staging with strong depth-haze layering, a small subject in a vast hazy environment, deep atmospheric falloff; painterly depth. Texture/render: minimalist near-silhouette forms, soft grain, volumetric fog, subtle rim-separation; restrained detail, atmosphere over rendering. Composition+motion: a tiny silhouette in a vast desaturated haze, dread built through scale and emptiness, slow lonely motion; one atmospheric shift (fog drift, a distant shape); the liminal emptiness and monochrome haze carry the unease.

avoid: NO the specific Inside/Limbo boy character or named game creatures/set-pieces. NO franchise-specific imagery. NO saturated color, NO bright cheerful light, NO cluttered detail, NO 3D-realistic render.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Makoto Shinkai sky-light emotional-scale lineage (hyper-detailed cel grammar, original subjects only). dna: Stylized illustration frame in the Takehiko Inoue Vagabond sumi-e ink-brush lineage (manga ink grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: contemporary seinen ink-brush manga as an illustration doctrine. Named anchor: Inoue ink-brush negative space — kinetic sumi-e brushstroke, expressive dry-brush, vast white. Signature light: no rendered light — the white paper is light, the ink is shadow; form emerges from confident brush-value, dry-brush texture carrying the mid-tones; stark and expressive. Color/grade: sumi-e monochrome — black ink on white paper, occasional single restrained wash accent; the ink gradation from wet-black to dry-grey is the whole palette. Lens/optics: flat 2D, kinetic asymmetric brush-composition, vast negative space (white paper), a focal ink-mass against emptiness; no lens. Texture/render: expressive confident sumi-e brushstroke — wet-black to dry-brush gradation, splatter and dry-drag texture, calligraphic energy in every line; the brushwork itself is alive and gestural. Composition+motion: a kinetic focal ink-mass against vast white negative space, one decisive gestural stroke carrying the motion, splatter-energy on the peak; the confident brush-gesture and negative-space tension carry the intensity.
anchor: Inoue sumi-e ink-brush: kinetic wet-to-dry brushstroke, black ink as shadow on white-paper light, vast negative space, calligraphic gestural energy, splatter texture, decisive focal ink-mass.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Vagabond's sumi-e ink-brush gesture and negative-space intensity onto the chosen world; use for a raw, expressive, brush-gesture high-intensity beat.
avoid: NO Musashi, NO Kojiro, NO named Vagabond character. NO franchise-specific historical figures or settings. NO clean digital vector line, NO photoreal render, NO saturated color palette, NO 3D.
